injuries.detail: day-to-day
This will mark Nye's sixth straight game on the sidelines. Her next chance to play will come Thursday against the Mystics.

Jones, who hasn't yet been cleared for 5-on-5 drills, continues to make significant progress in her rehab from right knee surgery. The star center still doesn't carry a firm timetable for a return, but it sounds like she'll have a chance to return at some point in early July. Until Jones is ready to return, Naz Hillmon should continue to play a big role in the frontcourt alongside Angel Reese.
Westbeld will be held out of Sunday's contest to preserve her eligibility moving forward. The developmental player's next opportunity to suit up will come Friday in Las Vegas.
Carrington is still rehabbing from foot surgery and continues to get ruled out a full day in advance of tipoff, suggesting she's still not close. Carrington should be viewed as week-to-week.

The season-ending knee injury is a devastating development for Jackson and the Sky, as the 25-year-old forward was well on her way to a career-best campaign. Through Chicago's first four games, Jackson averaged 18.0 points, 4.8 rebounds, 2.0 assists, 1.8 blocks and 1.5 three-pointers in 29.5 minutes per contest. After Jackson's early exit in Sunday's contest, Aicha Coulibaly opened the second half with the starters and looks to be the most likely candidate to move into the top unit for the Sky's next game Wednesday versus the Wings.
day-to-day
day-to-day
Van Lith continues to manage a left ankle contusion and will miss a sixth consecutive contest. Her next chance to play comes July 2, when the Sun host the Wings.

Verona was not listed on Saturday's injury report, but has since been added, which is standard procedure for players on developmental deals. Verano's next chance to play will come Thursday in Connecticut.
This will be Smith's third consecutive game on the sidelines. Her next chance to play will come Thursday in Connecticut.
Sims continues to get ruled out 24 hours in advance of tipoff, indicating she hasn't made much progress. Sims should be viewed on a week-to-week basis moving forward.
Sowah will be held out of Sunday's game in order to preserve her eligibility under a developmental contract. Her next opportunity to play will come in Atlanta on Saturday.
Prechtel is signed to a developmental pact and will sit out Sunday's contest in an effort to preserve her eligibility. Her next chance to suit up will come Saturday in Atlanta.

Rupert signed a multi-year contract with Golden State during the offseason and missed the start of training camp due to an overseas commitment. Her status for the 2026 regular season now appears to be in jeopardy due to her pregnancy. The 24-year-old made 21 regular-season appearances (11 starts) for the Valkyries last year and averaged 9.3 points, 3.9 rebounds and 1.6 assists in 23.1 minutes per game. If Rupert is sidelined in 2026, Janelle Salaun, Kiah Stokes and Laeticia Amihere are candidates to see increased work.
Cunningham missed a second straight practice Tuesday and remains day-to-day. Fortunately for the Fever, the team is off until July 5.

Clark was limited to individual work Monday, and her status remains the same Tuesday. The team will continue to exercise caution moving forward, and her status for Sunday's game against the Aces looks to be in doubt.
Pissott will hit the inactive list Saturday in an effort to preserve her eligibility under a developmental contract. Her next chance to play will come in Las Vegas on Sunday, July 5.
Wilson was previously listed as questionable by the Aces. With this news, Cheyenne Parker-Tyus, NaLyssa Smith and Stephanie Talbot are all candidates for increased playing time.

Barker has yet to make her WNBA debut and has been sidelined for most of the regular season due to a concussion and, currently, a right leg injury. Her next chance to play is Friday against the Sky, but she can be considered doubtful for that contest unless the Liberty indicate otherwise.
Evans will remain on the inactive list Tuesday and has yet to make her season debut due to a left leg injury. Her next chance to play will come Friday against Chicago.
Martin will sit out Saturday's contest in an effort to preserve her eligibility while signed to a developmental contract. Her next chance to play will come against the Storm on Monday, July 6.
Re-evaluated does not mean will return, so Brink's absence could extend beyond this two-week window. Jihyun Park, Emma Cannon and Laura Ziegler will continue to pick up the slack in Brink's absence.

This is a brutal blow for Plum and the Sparks. Based on this initial timetable, the earliest possible return would be July 28 against the Liberty. Through 12 appearances, she has posted averages of 23.9 points and 6.4 assists on 52.7 percent shooting from the field. In her absence, the Sparks will need to lean more on Ariel Atkins and Erica Wheeler.
Juhasz continues to recover from a right mid-foot sprain. The Lynx did not provide a target date for her return, but she can expected back in the lineup in the near future.

Collier underwent surgery on both of her ankles in early January and then had another operation on her left one in March. She's progressing towards a return, but the Lynx haven't provided a target date just yet.
This will mark Hamzova's fourth consecutive game on the inactive list. Her next chance to play will come Friday in New York.

Cechova suffered a non-contact injury during Thursday's 90-86 win over Dallas and required assistance off the floor. After undergoing an MRI, she was diagnosed with a torn ACL, ending her season. The 21-year-old rookie appeared in three games, and she will finish the 2026 campaign by averaging 8.3 points, 3.7 rebounds, 0.3 steals and 0.7 blocks across 17.3 minutes. With Cechova sidelined, Anastasiia Kosu could be first in line to see more action.
out
out

Nogic had her contract temporarily suspended June 20 and will now be out for the remainder of the WNBA season, as she's slated to compete overseas in Russia with an eye on leading the Serbian national team in the EuroBasket 2027 qualifiers. The guard appeared in 16 games (seven starts) for Phoenix, averaging 10.4 points, 1.6 assists and 1.1 rebounds in 20.2 minutes per contest. With Nogic out of the fold for the foreseeable future, Alexa Held stands to continue benefiting from expanded opportunities in the backcourt.

Brochant finished with three points (1-4 FG, 1-3 3Pt), two rebounds, two assists, and three steals over 28 minutes in the win. For now, she should be considered questionable for Thursday's game versus the Storm. If she needs to miss time, that would open up minutes for Marta Suarez and DeWanna Bonner.

Akoa Makani will miss a fourth consecutive contest due to a left hamstring injury, and her next chance to play arrives Thursday against Seattle. Alexa Held has started three straight games and is likely to stick with the first unit in Akoa Makani's absence Saturday.

This is a tough blow for Whitcomb and the Mercury as she will now miss the first month or so of the WNBA season. Whitcomb served as a part-time starter during her first season in Phoenix last year, averaging 9.1 points, 2.6 rebounds and 2.5 assists in 23.9 minutes per game across 43 regular-season appearances (20 starts). Kiana Williams could see an uptick in minutes with this news.
This will mark Feagin's third straight game on the sidelines. Her next chance to play will come Saturday in Seattle.

Samuelson will miss a second consecutive contest due to an injured middle finger on her left hand. Her next chance to play will come Saturday against Seattle. With the 31-year-old guard sidelined, Teja Oblak, Frieda Buhner and Holly Winterburn are candidates for increased playing time.
Horston will miss a fifth straight game Saturday. Without Horston, Jade Melbourne will presumably remain in the starting lineup.
Mair is set to hit the inactive list for a second consecutive contest while the Storm manage her eligibility under a developmental pact. Her next chance to play will come Thursday in Phoenix.

Magbegor was given a 6-to-8-week timetable in April due to a right foot injury, and she's progressing toward a return to game action. However, there's still no clear timetable for her return, so the 26-year-old can be considered week-to-week until the Storm offer another update on her progress.
out
Sykes is dealing with a plantar fascia injury and will remain sidelined for the Tempo. In the meantime, Toronto will likely lean more on Julie Allemand and Kia Nurse.

The injury was previously considered a Grade 2 sprain, so Rice is trending in the right direction. According to Copeland, Rice will be re-evaluated in the coming weeks. Julie Allemand will likely see an uptick in minutes in the meantime.

Amoore exited to the locker room with 5:53 remaining in the second quarter and won't return during the second half. Lucy Olsen started the third quarter and will likely see increased burn the rest of the way. Amoore's next chance to play will come Thursday against the Dream.
